goal: Land Phase 3 of the visual redesign — additive --mt-* token namespace, global chrome re-skin (header, bottom-nav, footer), card-back visual on the daily and question-draw selection screens, and a new flip animation timing with reveal glow.
done: Appended a Phase-3 :root block to css/theme-tokens.css with the --mt-* token namespace (plum/rose/gold/shadow/glow/radius/nav/streak — additive only, no override of existing --color-* tokens). Created css/phase-3-redesign.css containing §1 global chrome rules (site-header, bottom-nav, site-footer) and §2 card-back override scoped narrowly to body[data-page='daily'] .card .back and body[data-page='question-draw'] .card .back. Edited the flip animation block in css/styles.css to use var(--mt-flip-duration, 650ms) with var(--mt-flip-easing, cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1)) and added @keyframes mt-reveal-glow that runs once on .card.revealed .back (animation, not transition — transition can't fade from one static value to another on a single class toggle). Wired phase-3-redesign.css into all 12 EN+TH pages via a stylesheet link after phase-2-1-logo.css. cp canonical → www/ and npx cap sync ios completed; md5 canonical==www verified for the three CSS files plus index.html and th/index.html; phase-3 link confirmed present in ios mirror.
affects: all 12 EN+TH pages — site-header, bottom-nav, site-footer re-skinned with --mt-* tokens
daily.html and th/daily.html plus the question-draw selection board — card-back now deep plum-to-rose with gold inner border
card-flip timing across all reading flows — duration up to 650ms with cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) easing, one-shot reveal glow on .card.revealed
notes: User's spec named the flip-state class as .card.flipped; the JS in js/reading.js actually toggles .card.revealed, so the new animation rule targets .card.revealed .back. User's spec referenced share/share.js for the poster canvas; the file is share/poster.js. Poster canvas palette (dozens of hex values for the old dark-navy palette — #0f1429, #f8d77a, #f7f4ee, #aab0c9, etc) was NOT touched this round — flagged to user for a follow-up phase. Per-screen redesigns (per-page backgrounds, result/profile/full-reading layout work) deliberately deferred to later phases. desktop-unchanged is NOT claimed: site-header rule applies globally now so desktop pastel chrome is affected.
verified: md5 canonical == www for css/theme-tokens.css, css/phase-3-redesign.css, css/styles.css, index.html, th/index.html
grep phase-3-redesign.css === 1 in all 12 canonical pages and the corresponding ios mirror pages
EN/TH css link-set parity diff: index.html vs th/index.html identical phase sequence
cap sync ios completed in 106ms, no plugin errors
working tree was reset with git checkout -- www/ ios/ before edits to discard pre-existing mirror drift
